{{Quote|There's nothing like this on Endor.|Teebo|<ref name="Night of the Stranger" />}}
A '''capsule''' carried the [[Stranger]] to the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]]. In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], two [[Moon/Legends|moons]] near Endor [[Eclipse/Legends|aligned]], and a rift in [[Realspace/Legends|space]] opened, through which the capsule traveled. The capsule fell toward the [[Forest/Legends|forested]] moon, and the young [[Ewok/Legends|Ewoks]] [[Wicket Wystri Warrick/Legends|Wicket Wystri Warrick]] and [[Teebo/Legends|Teebo]] saw it and mistook it for a [[Comet/Legends|Comet]]. When the capsule touched down, the Stranger emerged, and the craft was located by the two Ewoks and their friends [[Kneesaa a Jari Kintaka/Legends|Kneesaa a Jari Kintaka]] and [[Latara/Legends|Latara]]. As the Ewoks began to carry the capsule back to their [[Bright Tree Village/Legends|village]], it illuminated, spun rapidly, and vanished.

==History==
[[File:Ship2.jpg|thumb|left|200px|The Ewok children come upon the capsule.]]
In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],<ref name="Date note">{{InsiderCite|23|Star Wars Publications Timeline|''Star Wars'' Publications Timeline}} dates the events of the [[Ewoks (television series)|''Ewoks'']] animated series, which include the appearance of the capsule in "[[Night of the Stranger]]," to three [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after the events of [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']], which corresponds to [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.</ref> the a capsule carried the [[Stranger]] to the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]]. When the capsule landed, the Stranger made his way to the [[Dulok/Legends|Dulok]] swamps, but not before witnessing the [[Ewok/Legends|Ewoks]], [[Wicket Wystri Warrick/Legends|Wicket Wystri Warrick]] and [[Teebo/Legends|Teebo]] coming across the ship. Wicket and Teebo, with help from [[Latara/Legends|Latara]] and [[Kneesaa a Jari Kintaka/Legends|Kneesaa a Jari Kintaka]], tried to carry the capsule back to [[Bright Tree Village/Legends|Bright Tree Village]]. Wicket took a piece of it and attached it to his [[Belt of Honor]] as proof of his quest towards becoming a full-fledged Ewok warrior.<ref name="Night of the Stranger" />

[[File:Ship3.jpg|thumb|200px|The capsule, shortly before vanishing]]
As the Ewoks walked, the capsule illuminated bright [[Color/Legends|purple]] light and began to spin in a circle. The speed increased until the capsule was a blur, and the Ewoks stepped back. The capsule then disintegrated into a cloud of smoke, leaving no trace except for the "trophy" on Wicket's belt.<ref name="Night of the Stranger" />

==Appearances==
*{{Ewoks|Night of the Stranger}} {{1st}}
